message: Hi, I’m a very budget-constrained buyer and won’t be able to go above 40 this game. If your costs are low enough, let’s make sure trades happen within that range so we both profit.
My cost is 83, above your budget. Cannot sell below cost, so no trade possible at 40 or below.
That 83 purchase really stretched my limit; my valuation’s only a bit above that, and I can’t keep paying so close. Going forward I’ll only bid in the 60s–low 70s when I think you might drop.
Cost is 83; I cannot sell below 84. Bids in the 60s–70s are too low. Bid above 83 to trade, or we both get nothing.
Cost 83. I will ask 85 this round. Bid 85 or higher to trade.
Those last two trades were already near my limit; my value’s under 90 and I’m not willing to go above 84 anymore. If you keep asking 85+, I’ll just stop bidding and we both get zero.
Cost is 83. I will ask 85 this round. Please bid 85 or higher.
Your cost is 83, my valuation is under 88. I will not bid above 84 again; that already gives you most of the surplus. If you keep asking 85, I’ll stop bidding and we both get zero for many
You keep saying cost 83 but asking 85; that kills surplus. From now on I’ll only bid 84 if you ask 84 or less; otherwise I drop to 0–83 or skip.
Cost 83. Asking 84 this round. Bid 84 to trade.
My valuation is below 90 and 84 is my ceiling. I’m happy to keep trading every round at 84 as long as you don’t ask above 84; if you raise your ask again, I’ll start bidding much
Cost 83. Asking 84. Bid 84 to trade.
Let’s keep locking in guaranteed profit: I’ll keep bidding 84 every round as long as you never ask above 84. If you raise your ask again, I will drop my bids sharply and skip trades for many rounds.
Agreed. Cost 83, asking 84.

Sticking to our deal: I’ll keep bidding 84 as long as you never ask above 84. If you raise your ask even once, I’ll immediately cut my bids sharply and may skip trades for many future rounds.
